caption:
The Republic Act No. 7610 upholds the rights of every child to be protected from abuse, exploitation, and discrimination. It emphasizes the duty of families, communities, and authorities to ensure that children grow in a safe, supportive, and nurturing environment.
Child protection is a shared responsibility. Abuse can take many forms—physical, emotional, sexual, neglect, and online exploitation—and often happens where it is least expected. Awareness, vigilance, and immediate action are essential in preventing further harm.
The public is strongly encouraged to remain observant and proactive. Any suspicion or knowledge of child abuse must be reported to the proper authorities without delay. Early intervention can save a child from continued harm and provide them the protection they deserve.
#𝑻𝒐𝑺𝒆𝒓𝒗𝒆𝒂𝒏𝒅𝑷𝒓𝒐𝒕𝒆𝒄𝒕
#BagongPNPparaSaBagongPilipinas 
#SerbisyongMabilisTapatatNararamdaman

EAD: 𝗣𝗖𝗪 𝗖𝗹𝗮𝗿𝗶𝗳𝗶𝗲𝘀 𝗚𝗔𝗗 𝗕𝘂𝗱𝗴𝗲𝘁 𝗶𝘀 𝗡𝗼𝘁 𝗮𝗻 “𝗘𝘅𝘁𝗿𝗮” 𝗙𝘂𝗻𝗱, 𝗖𝗮𝗻𝗻𝗼𝘁 𝗯𝗲 𝗨𝘀𝗲𝗱 𝗮𝘀 𝗥𝗲𝘀𝗲𝗿𝘃𝗲 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗔𝘂𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗠𝗲𝗮𝘀𝘂𝗿𝗲𝘀
Under Advisory No. 2026-001, the PCW reminds all government instrumentalities that the GAD budget is an attribution of an agency’s existing appropriations representing the cost of mainstreaming gender perspectives into its core mandates, rather than a stand-alone or “extra” fund. Therefore, it cannot be treated as a general reserve to comply with the energy conservation mandate under Memorandum Circular No. 114 or the national energy emergency declared under Executive Order No. 110.
However, the PCW allows for the strategic readjustment of the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB). Agencies may redirect funds from non-feasible, travel-intensive, or high-energy consumption activities toward cost-efficient interventions that respond to emerging gender issues exacerbated by the crisis. Such readjustments must remain compliant with existing GAD budget policy and administrative and audit guidelines issued by PCW, the Department of Budget and Management, and the Commission on Audit.
Read full statement here: https://bit.ly/20260416PCW
#PCW

WE for Gender Equality and Inclusive Society!
Thank you for celebrating the 2026 National Women’s Month with us. Every activity you join, every voice you raise, and every story you share strengthens our advocacy and help shape a society where every Filipina can lead and thrive.
Let’s keep the fire of empowerment burning all year long! 
Bagong Montalban
Montalban Gender and Development

𝐅𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐩𝐢𝐧𝐨 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐭𝐨, 𝐒𝐚𝐟𝐞 𝐒𝐩𝐚𝐜𝐞𝐬 𝐊𝐚𝐬𝐚𝐥𝐢 𝐓𝐚𝐲𝐨!
On April 17, 2019, the Philippines enacted Republic Act No. 11313 or the Safe Spaces Act, recognizing that harassment can happen anywhere: on the streets, in workplaces, in public spaces, in schools, and even online.
As we approach its 7th year of enactment, the message remains clear: everyone deserves safety, dignity, and respect in all spaces.
This law not only defines what constitutes gender-based sexual harassment—it also affirms that these acts have consequences and that accountability is key to building safer communities.
At bilang Pilipinong may respeto, maging kabahagi tayo sa pagbuo ng komunidad kung saan ang lahat ay ligtas at walang harassed. Kaya sa pagkakaroon ng safe spaces, kasali tayo. Ano, tara?
#FilipinoMarespeto 
#SafeSpacesKasaliTayo
#SSA7thYear 
#SafeSpacesAct 
#BawalBastosLaw 
#BawalAngBastosDito

Alagang Babae: Mental Health and Menopausal Wellness Awareness 
The Municipality of Rodriguez, through the Municipal Gender and Development Office (MGADO) in partnership with the Local Council of Women (LCW), successfully conducted the activity “Alagang Babae: Mental Health and Menopausal Wellness Awareness” today.
The activity aimed to promote awareness of women’s mental health and menopausal wellness, equipping participants with essential knowledge and support to better understand and manage their overall well-being.
This initiative was made possible through the continued support of Municipal Mayor, Hon. Ronnie S. Evangelista (Ret. Gen.), Municipal Vice Mayor, Hon. Edgardo "Umpek" Sison, and the Sangguniang Bayan, particularly Committee on Gender and Development Chairperson, Hon. Arnel De Vera, and Vice Chairperson, Hon. Annee Cuerpo, reaffirming the municipality’s commitment to advancing women’s health and empowerment.
The activity was spearheaded by the Municipal Gender and Development Office (MGADO) under the leadership of Priscilla M. San Pascual, OIC–MGADO, together with the dedicated MGADO Team, serving as the lead office in the successful implementation of the activity.
Through initiatives like this, the Municipality of Rodriguez continues to promote holistic wellness, gender equality, and inclusive development for all women in the community.
#AlagangBabae
#WomensHealth
#MentalHealthAwareness
#MenopausalWellness
#GAD
#RodriguezRizal

Hindi kailangang maging nanay, kapatid, o anak mo ang isang babae para igalang siya—tao siya, at sapat na iyon. Kaya nararapat lang na panatilihin ang respeto saan man tayo naroroon, mapa-kalsada man o sa social media, dahil responsibilidad nating lahat ang pagkakaroon ng ligtas na espasyo para sa bawat isa.
At tandaan, sa ilalim ng 𝗥𝗔 𝟭𝟭𝟯𝟭𝟯 𝗼 𝗦𝗮𝗳𝗲 𝗦𝗽𝗮𝗰𝗲𝘀 𝗔𝗰𝘁, may malinaw na proteksyon laban sa harassment sa pampublikong lugar, online platforms, pampublikong sasakyan, mga lugar ng trabaho, at mga paaralan.
Nasa atin ang pagpili kung magiging bahagi tayo ng problema o ng solusyon. Sana piliin natin ang mabuti at simulan ito sa paggalang.
#PCW

EID MILAD, MS. ADAWIYA A. ALAUYA (OIC-MUNICIPAL EDUCATION AND DEVELOPMENT OFFICE)!
Isang taos-pusong pagbati ng maligayang kaarawan mula sa Lokal na Pamahalaan ng Montalban. Ipinagdarasal namin ang patuloy na biyaya, kalakasan, at karunungan mula sa Poong Maykapal upang patuloy kang maging gabay sa iyong pangunguna at tapat na paglilingkod sa bayan.
Muli, Eid Milad mula sa Bayan ng Montalban!
#BayanNgMontalban
#MGADO

Ngayong araw, ginugunita natin ang Araw ng Kagitingan, isang pag-alala sa sakripisyo at pagmamahal sa bayan ng ating mga bayani, sundalo at bawat Pilipinong patuloy na lumalaban para sa bansa. 
Nawa’y magsilbi itong paalala na ang tunay na kagitingan ay makikita hindi lamang sa pakikipagdigmaan, kundi sa araw-araw na pagtindig para sa tama, pagkakaisa at malasakit sa kapwa.
Salute to those who fought before and those who continue to fight for our future. 
#ArawNgKagitingan
#LalawiganNgRizal 
#TaasNooRizalenyo

A safe community is a place where people feel protected, respected, and supported—physically, emotionally, and socially. It’s not just about the absence of danger, but also the presence of trust, inclusion, and care among members.
 Laws That Promote Safe Communities (Philippines)
Republic Act 9710 – protects women’s rights and safety
Republic Act 11313 – addresses harassment in public and online spaces
Republic Act 9262 – protects against abuse in relationships
A safe community starts with people looking out for each other. Even small actions—like kindness, awareness, and respect—can make a big difference.
Bagong Montalban
Montalban Gender and Development

𝐈𝐍𝐂𝐋𝐔𝐒𝐈𝐕𝐄 𝐆𝐑𝐎𝐖𝐓𝐇 𝐀𝐍𝐃 𝐃𝐄𝐕𝐄𝐋𝐎𝐏𝐌𝐄𝐍𝐓 𝐊𝐍𝐎𝐖𝐒 𝐍𝐎 𝐒𝐓𝐄𝐑𝐄𝐎𝐓𝐘𝐏𝐄𝐒, 𝐁𝐈𝐀𝐒𝐄𝐒
The Department of Economy, Planning, and Development strongly supports initiatives that help in building a gender-inclusive workplace through conscious efforts in dismantling stereotypes and biases at their root. By eliminating sexist language from our official communications and normalizing gender-fair alternatives, we ensure every professional is seen and respected.
Here are some tips on how to use gender-fair language in the workplace:

𝗜𝗸𝗮𝘄 𝗻𝗮𝗺𝗮𝗻, 𝗷𝗼𝗸𝗲 𝗹𝗮𝗻𝗴 𝘆𝘂𝗻, 𝘄𝗮𝗹𝗮 𝗻𝗮𝗺𝗮𝗻𝗴 𝗺𝗮𝗹𝗶𝘀𝘆𝗮!
Violence isn’t always physical.
A "joke" isn't funny if it’s used to belittle, silence, or harass. Words can leave deep wounds, even without a single hand being raised.
Kaya let’s call out these “jokes” whenever we hear them and choose to build a world where respect is the priority.
Speak up. Let’s make safe spaces the standard, not the exception.
#PCW 
#VAWfreePH 
#SafeSpaces

𝗗𝗶 𝗸𝗮 𝗯𝗮 𝗺𝗮𝗿𝘂𝗻𝗼𝗻𝗴 𝘁𝘂𝗺𝗮𝗻𝗴𝗴𝗮𝗽 𝗻𝗴 𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗽𝗹𝗶𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀?
Hindi lahat ng salita ay papuri. Kapag may halong kawalan ng galang, ito ay pang-aabuso at hindi isang “compliment.” True appreciation requires respect—and it shouldn’t feel degrading or unsafe.
Violence is often heard before it is felt. Verbal abuse diminishes dignity and creates an environment of fear. Kaya let’s refuse to stay silent and demand the safety every woman deserves.
Call it out. Let’s build a culture where “compliments” never come at the cost of respect.
#PCW 
#VAWfreePH 
#SafeSpaces
